shortDescription: Home Affairs · Partner visa · Onshore/offshore · Relationship & health

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current partner visa fees (AUD ,850+ approx for primary applicant) on homeaffairs.gov.au before quoting.

alert.title: Two-stage partner visa
alert.body: Subclass 309/100 (offshore) or 820/801 (onshore) are two-stage processes. Choose pathway based on where applicant is at time of application.

about.label: Description
about.value: Partner visa for spouse or de facto partner of Australian citizen, permanent resident, or eligible NZ citizen. Provisional visa (309/820) then permanent (100/801) after relationship assessment period.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Married or de facto 12+ months · Genuine relationship · Health and character · Sponsor eligibility · Mutual commitment

about.label: Onshore vs offshore
about.value: 820/801 if partner in Australia on eligible visa. 309/100 if outside Australia. Bridging visa rules apply onshore.

about.label: After approval
about.value: Stage 1 provisional visa; Stage 2 permanent after waiting period and continued relationship evidence.

eligibility.criterion: Qualifying relationship (marriage or 12+ months de facto)
Sponsor is eligible Australian citizen/PR/eligible NZ citizen

eligibility.note: Assess partner location and visa status

redFlagsBanner: If refused on relationship grounds, rebuild chronological evidence before reapplication—do not resubmit same weak package.

redFlags.title: Weak relationship evidence
redFlags.description: Few photos, no cohabitation proof, inconsistent timeline.
redFlags.fix: Chronological narrative; communication logs; joint finances; visit evidence

redFlags.title: Wrong pathway (onshore/offshore)
redFlags.description: Applied onshore without eligible visa status.
redFlags.fix: Verify partner visa and location before lodgement

redFlags.title: De facto without 12 months cohabitation
redFlags.fix: Marriage registration or 12+ months shared residence proof

redFlags.title: Health/character issues
redFlags.description: Medical conditions or undisclosed criminal history.
redFlags.fix: Complete health exam early; disclose all history

redFlags.title: Sponsor ineligible
redFlags.description: Sponsor has prior sponsorship debt or ineligible status.
redFlags.fix: Verify sponsor eligibility on Home Affairs website

faqs.q: Can we guarantee partner visa approval?
faqs.a: No. Genuine relationships can still face refusals if evidence is insufficient.
309/100 offshore; 820/801 onshore when partner holds eligible visa in Australia.

faqs.q: Can partner work on provisional visa?
faqs.a: Generally full work rights on stage 1—verify grant conditions.
Typically 2 years on provisional before stage 2 assessment—verify current processing.
Yes if legally registered; provide certified translation if needed.
Recent relationships need stronger evidence of development before marriage/de facto.
Sponsor provides assurance of support; explain obligations to client.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
Complex cases may need registered migration agent in Australia
proTips: Build relationship timeline from first meeting
Collect police certificates for all required countries
postApproval: Remind on ongoing relationship evidence duty

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial Australia partner visa counselor content.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Onshore 820 requires eligible substantive visa—verify before lodgement fee.

donts: Do not submit fabricated relationship evidence
mistakes: Insufficient cohabitation proof for de facto
